question_text: When considering the running costs of household appliances, energy companies charge customers based on the amount of electrical energy consumed. Which of the following factors would increase the total energy cost of running an appliance?

options.label: A
options.text: Reducing the time the appliance is switched on

options.label: B
options.text: Increasing the power rating of the appliance and using it for longer periods

options.label: C
options.text: Using the appliance during cooler months only

options.label: D
options.text: Decreasing the voltage supplied to the appliance

correct_answer: Increasing the power rating of the appliance and using it for longer periods
explanation: Energy cost is calculated using the formula: Energy = Power × Time. To increase the total energy cost, you need to increase either the power rating (how much energy the appliance uses per second) or the time it runs for, or both. A higher power rating and longer usage time both mean more electrical energy is consumed, resulting in higher costs.
